case_study.title: Astegolimab COPD — Phase 2b signal, Phase 3 miss
case_study.framing: Historical educational case study on biomarker-agnostic enrollment and Phase 2→3 divergence. Not a recommendation to resurrect the asset.

program.name: Astegolimab COPD program
program.moa: Anti-ST2 monoclonal antibody targeting the IL-33/ST2 pathway upstream of eosinophilic inflammation
program.cou_note: Educational GapForge case study — hypotheses for scientific discussion only
program.stall_summary: Phase 2b (ALIENTO) met primary endpoint on exacerbation reduction regardless of eosinophil count; confirmatory Phase 3 (ARNASA) did not achieve statistical significance despite a directional effect.

trials.primary_endpoint: Annualized exacerbation rate in moderate–very severe COPD with frequent exacerbations
trials.outcome_summary: Reported statistically significant reduction in annual exacerbation rates regardless of baseline blood eosinophil count.
Directional ~14.5% reduction in exacerbations reported but did not cross the statistical threshold for regulatory success in the confirmatory design.

hypotheses.claim: Enrolling regardless of blood eosinophil count may have diluted a true responder subgroup in Phase 3, despite a mechanistic rationale that ST2/IL-33 acts upstream of eosinophilic inflammation.
hypotheses.suggested_experiment: Re-analyse public COPD exacerbation-trial literature for eosinophil-stratified effect sizes on anti-IL-33/ST2 and related pathways; pre-specify biomarker-enriched primary populations or powered co-primaries in future designs.

literature_refs.title: Astegolimab COPD Phase 2b vs Phase 3 design discussion
literature_refs.note: Highlights all-comers vs biomarker enrichment tension

hypotheses.claim: Exacerbation-rate endpoints in heterogeneous COPD populations may require larger samples or different sensitivity assumptions when translating a Phase 2b signal into Phase 3 confirmation.
hypotheses.suggested_experiment: Power simulation using published Phase 2b effect sizes and Phase 3 variance estimates; compare all-comers vs enriched designs under realistic dropout.

hypotheses.claim: The true population-average treatment effect may be smaller than Phase 2b suggested, such that a clinically meaningful directional Phase 3 effect still fails statistical confirmation.
hypotheses.suggested_experiment: Bayesian re-estimation of effect size using public summaries; map minimal clinically important difference for exacerbation reduction against observed Phase 3 point estimates.

literature_refs.title: Astegolimab COPD Phase 2b vs Phase 3 design discussion
literature_refs.note: Directional Phase 3 effect without statistical win
